post.summary: Register a new user via the webapp's self-service flow.
post.description:
The body is a [`ProvisionArgs`] JSON payload. The request must carry `x-registration-timestamp` (Unix seconds) and `x-registration-signature` (hex-encoded HMAC-SHA256 of `{timestamp}.{body}`) headers; the controller's `[app] registration_secret` must match the secret used to sign.

Returns 404 if self-service registration is disabled by config.

AdminBoard.description: Enriched board info for admin use

device_count.description: Number of enabled devices using this board

user_id.description: User ID who submitted the job

user_uuid.description: User UUID who submitted the job

AdminUserInfo.description: User info as returned to an admin. Identity fields (name, email, address, phone) come from the OIDC provider; everything else is local. `linkage_broken` is set when the local row's OIDC subject no longer resolves in the OIDC provider - the admin should either relink the row or delete it.

linkage_broken.description: True when the OIDC account this row references could not be resolved.

oidc_sub.description: OIDC subject linking the user to their OIDC account

ApiKeyList.description: List of API keys for the current user

CreateBoardArgs.description: Arguments for creating a new board

CreateDeviceArgs.description: Arguments for creating a new device

board_id.description: Board ID to associate the device with

probe_id.description: USB probe identifier. Held as a raw `String` here (not [`ProbeId`]) because this is an untrusted input that we need to length-validate before wrapping; the handler converts it to `ProbeId` after validation.

CreateManufacturerArgs.description: Arguments for creating a new manufacturer

CreateMcuArgs.description: Arguments for creating a new MCU

CreateUserBody.description:
Body for `POST /admin/user`. The `mode` discriminator selects between:

- `"provision"`: create a new account from scratch. - `"link"`: attach to an account that already exists.

oneOf.description: Create a new account and link it.
Link to an account that already exists.

oidc_sub.description: OIDC subject of the existing account.

ExitStatus.description: Terminal outcome of a finished job.

oneOf.description: Job completed successfully (exit code 0 or equivalent).
Job ran to completion but indicated failure.
Job was cancelled by the user.
Job was cancelled by the system due to timeout.

GeneratedApiKey.description: API key returned upon successful creation

api_key.description: Key string that can be used for user auth

IdResponse.description: Generic response with just an ID

id.description: Database primary key of the created resource

JobAccepted.description: Response structure returned after successfully submitting a job

id.description: Unique identifier for the submitted job

upload_prefix.description: URL prefix for uploading job chunks

board_name.description: Name of the board this job targets

finished_at.description: Timestamp when job execution finished (if applicable)

started_at.description: Timestamp when job execution started (if applicable)

submitted_at.description: Timestamp when the job was submitted

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum execution time allowed for this job in seconds

cancelled.description: Jobs that were cancelled by the user

dispatched.description: Jobs dispatched to a board, awaiting start

timed_out.description: Jobs that were cancelled by the system because they exceeded their timeout

oneOf.description: Submitted but not yet assigned to an edge server.
Sent to an edge server; waiting for the edge server to start it.
Currently running on an edge server.

JobStatusView.description: The status of a job in the system.

oneOf.description: Job has been submitted but not yet assigned to a board.
Job has been dispatched to a board and is awaiting start.
Job was cancelled by the user.
Job was cancelled by the system because it exceeded its timeout.

account_id.description: Optional – defaults to caller’s personal account if omitted.

file_hash.description: SHA3-256 hash of the complete file as bytes (exactly 32 bytes)

job_type.description: Whether this is a regular run job (flash + logstream) or an interactive debug session.

logging_config.description:
How firmware logs should be captured.

**IMPORTANT:** Currently only RTT is supported!

Always required, including for [`onmcu_common::domain::JobType::Debug`] jobs. For Debug jobs the value is currently **stored but not honored** — log capture during debug sessions is not yet implemented. Once it is, the field will start taking effect without an API change. Requiring it from day one keeps the API forward-compatible and avoids an `Option` rippling through the rest of the codebase.

JobType.description:
Kind of job to execute on the edge server.

Discriminant `0` is the default for jobs migrated from pre-`0005` schemas, which were all [`JobType::Run`] by definition.

LoggingConfig.description: Source of firmware logs during a running job.

oneOf.description: RTT (Real-Time Transfer) over the debug probe.

PaginatedResponse_for_BoardInfo.description: Paginated response wrapper for list endpoints

total_count.description: Total number of items across all pages

PaginatedResponse_for_JobInfo.description: Paginated response wrapper for list endpoints

total_count.description: Total number of items across all pages

oneOf.description: Normal priority — the default for user-submitted jobs.

ProbeId.description:
Probe identifier as a `VID:PID:SerialNumber` USB string.

Uniquely identifies a USB debug probe attached to an edge server.

ReducedApiKey.description: DbApiKey with reduced number of fields

uuid.description: UUID to identify this PK regardless of the password

oidc_sub.description: New OIDC subject. Must reference an existing OIDC account.

RttConfig.description: Configuration for RTT logging. (Placeholder — fields TBD.)

SerialConfig.description: Configuration for serial/UART logging. (Placeholder — fields TBD.)

UpdateEdgeServerArgs.description: Arguments for updating an edge server

UpdateUserArgs.description: Body for `PUT /admin/user/{id}`. Onmcu-local fields only - identity is edited in the OIDC provider.

UploadLimits.description: Upload size limits for the system

max_chunk_size.description: Maximum size for a single chunk in bytes

max_file_size.description: Maximum total file size in bytes

UploadQuota.description: Upload quota information for a user

quota_limit.description: Maximum number of concurrent uploads allowed

UserData.description: Wrapper struct for the user data

SortDirection.description: Sort direction for paginated list queries.
